'''Rakaa IV''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] occupying the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> in the [[Rakaa system/Legends|Rakaa system]],<ref name="EAOC Rakaa" /> on the edge of<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="EAOC Rakaa" /> It was a [[Temperature/Legends|warm]] world of [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] and [[Forest/Legends|forests]], with surface [[Water/Legends|water]] also being present. The planet's [[Plant/Legends|flora]] included the [[nargk root]].<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

Rakaa IV was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Rakaan]]s, a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of large, [[Diet/Legends|predatory]], hive-based<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]].<ref name="LoneWolf">{{StoryCite|story=Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke|book=SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story}}</ref> The Rakaans had a complex life-cycle which involved [[transition]]ing through five different [[Gender/Legends|sexes]], with one of the phases involving taking care of their [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] in nesting chambers.<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

The natives of Rakaa IV rarely traveled beyond their homeworld.<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> One exception was the Rakaan [[Lud Chud]], who established a [[Crime lord/Legends|criminal empire]] in the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]]<ref name="Planets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Rakaa IV was first mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]'', which was authored by [[John Terra]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Planets3">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Rakaa [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Rakaa IV, in grid square J-12.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
